# cloudflare_workflow (Resource)

Accepted Permissions

- `Workers Scripts Read`
- `Workers Scripts Write`
- `Workers Tail Read`

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudflare_workflow" "example_workflow" {
  account_id = "account_id"
  workflow_name = "x"
  class_name = "x"
  script_name = "x"
  limits = {
    steps = 1
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `class_name` (String)
- `script_name` (String)
- `workflow_name` (String)

### Optional

- `account_id` (String)
- `limits` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--limits))

### Read-Only

- `created_on` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `instances` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instances))
- `is_deleted` (Number)
- `modified_on` (String)
- `name` (String)
- `terminator_running` (Number)
- `triggered_on` (String)
- `version_id` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `limits`

Optional:

- `steps` (Number)


<a id="nestedatt--instances"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instances`

Read-Only:

- `complete` (Number)
- `errored` (Number)
- `paused` (Number)
- `queued` (Number)
- `running` (Number)
- `terminated` (Number)
- `waiting` (Number)
- `waiting_for_pause` (Number)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
$ terraform import cloudflare_workflow.example '<account_id>/<workflow_name>'
```
